definition: Post-2009 buy-the-dip success (recovery in 6-18 months) reflects ZIRP/QE/fiscal support, not a structural tendency. The pre-2009 and cross-country base rate for >20% drawdown recovery is ~52-60 months. Behavioral conditioning over 15+ years may partially self-validate through reflexive buying pressure.
evidence: Pre-2009 recovery base rate of ~52-60 months for >20% drawdowns
15+ years of reinforced buy-the-dip behavior as Pavlovian conditioning
open_questions: Whether behavioral response creates enough buying pressure to self-validate even without structural supports
Whether removal of ZIRP/QE regime breaks the conditioning
